subject: [PATCH 4/4] mm: align file-backed mmap to exec folio order in thp_get_unmapped_area

reviews.author: WANG Rui
reviews.summary: Identified an issue where even when the code segment satisfies PMD_SIZE, it still doesn't end up at a PMD-aligned virtual address. Suggested skipping the PMD_SIZE == folio_sz case to avoid calling __thp_get_unmapped_area() twice with the same parameters.
reviews.raw_body:
> +	if (exec_folio_order()) {
> +		unsigned long folio_size = PAGE_SIZE << exec_folio_order();
> +
> +		ret = __thp_get_unmapped_area(filp, addr, len, off, flags,
> +					      folio_size, vm_flags);
> +		if (ret)
> +			return ret;
> +	}
> +

I noticed that even when the code segment of a user-space shared library
satisfies PMD_SIZE (32MB), it still doesn\u2019t end up at a PMD-aligned virtual
address. This might be the fallback you mentioned. Adjusting p_align in the
ld.so ELF loader does work, though it also avoids extremely large PMD_SIZE
values (capped at \u226432M).

It would probably be better to skip the PMD_SIZE == folio_sz case here,
so we don\u2019t end up calling __thp_get_unmapped_area() twice with the same
parameters.

Thanks,
Rui

2026-03-10.patch_summary: This patch series addresses an issue where shared libraries loaded by ld.so via mmap() get 64K-aligned virtual addresses, preventing contpte mapping even when large folios are allocated. The patch introduces a fallback alignment in thp_get_unmapped_area_vmflags() that tries PAGE_SIZE << exec_folio_order() alignment (2M on arm64 64K pages) when PMD_SIZE alignment fails.
